specification.topic-statement: The agent list command queries the database for all agents in the current session and displays them as a formatted tree showing their hierarchy, status, and count.

scope.in-scope: Database query for session agents, breadth-first tree traversal for display ordering, per-agent line formatting, depth-based indentation, status indicators, and scrollback output.
scope.boundary: How agents are stored and retrieved from the database is handled by the agent database layer. This topic invokes that layer but does not implement it.

behavior.database-query: All agents belonging to the current session are retrieved from the database. The query returns both running and dead agents. Any arguments supplied to the command are ignored.
behavior.tree-traversal:
The agents are arranged into a tree using breadth-first traversal. Root agents — those with no parent — are enqueued first. For each dequeued agent, its children are found by scanning the full agent list for agents whose parent identifier matches the current agent's identifier. Those children are enqueued after their parent, along with a depth value one greater than the parent's depth. Root agents have depth zero.
This traversal produces a display order in which each agent appears immediately before its subtree.
Finding children of each agent requires scanning the entire agent list. No index is maintained. The traversal cost grows quadratically with the number of agents in the session.
behavior.line-formatting:
Each agent is formatted as a single line appended to the current agent's scrollback.
Root agents (depth zero) receive a marker prefix character: an asterisk if the agent is also the current active agent, or a space otherwise. They are followed by their full UUID and the literal label " - root".
Non-root agents (depth one or greater) are indented with four spaces per depth level (counting from one), then prefixed with "+-- ", followed by their full UUID.
Dead agents have " [dead]" appended after their UUID.
Each line is assembled into a 256-byte fixed buffer. If a combination of indentation and UUID content exceeds this limit, the line is silently truncated.
behavior.header-and-summary: A header line reading "Agent Hierarchy:" is appended to the scrollback before the agent lines. After all agent lines, a summary line is appended in the format "N running, M dead", where N and M are counts accumulated during the traversal.
behavior.data-source:
The display is built from the database query result, not from the in-memory agent list. Agents that have been reaped (and thus removed from memory) do not appear. Agents from other sessions do not appear.
Using the database as the source of truth means the display reflects the persisted state, including agents that may not be loaded in the current REPL instance.
behavior.error-behavior: If the database query fails, an error is returned and no output is appended to the scrollback. If any scrollback append fails, an error is returned immediately.
